summary: Iteration 19 — Validated the launcher persistence fix for PyInstaller-frozen builds (BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH + BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT now pinned into USER_DATA_DIR so Setup Wizard does NOT re-run on every relaunch). Created /app/backend/tests/test_launcher_persistence_iter19.py with 15 assertions across 7 test classes covering: (1) FROZEN + clean env -> launcher._load_env() sets BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH = USER_DATA_DIR/system_config.json and BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT = USER_DATA_DIR/app, and the resulting cfg path is NOT under BACKEND_DIR (the _MEIxxxx temp dir); (2) FROZEN + explicit override -> setdefault semantics preserved for BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH, BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT, AND partial overrides (one set, the other defaulted); (3) DEV mode (sys.frozen unset / explicitly False) -> launcher does NOT set either env var, sandbox keeps using /app/backend/native/system_config.json; (4) USER_DATA_DIR is created on disk before env vars are exported (writable parent verified with a probe file); (5) END-TO-END persistence simulation — fresh USER_DATA_DIR, ConfigManager reloaded after setting env, save_config({setup_complete:True, license_status..., users...}) -> system_config.json appears at USER_DATA_DIR/system_config.json -> importlib.reload(native.config) -> new ConfigManager() reads back setup_complete=True with the SAME instance_id, license key, and users (Setup Wizard would NOT re-run); paths.{data_root,local_trivia,assets,generated} all default under USER_DATA_DIR/app; (6) PRD.md contains the new 'INSTALLER STATE MUST PERSIST IN USER_DATA_DIR — NEVER IN _MEIxxxx' section (line 91, immediately after INSTALLER MUST KILL on line 51); (7) backend /api/ returns 200 (hot-reload didn't break the dev server). All 15/15 PASS. Existing phase 9.1 regression (/app/backend/tests/test_phase9_1_updates_native.py) shows the IDENTICAL pre-existing 7 failed + 4 errored as iter-18 (VERSION.txt=32.0.0-alpha.11 > fixture 31.1.0, and unrelated /api/native/sync/status 404) — no new regressions introduced by the iter-19 launcher change. Alpha.11 customers who upgrade to alpha.12 will see the Setup Wizard ONE FINAL TIME because their prior state lived in _MEIxxxx and was wiped on the alpha.11 close — that's acceptable and called out in the RCA section.
action_items: PRE-EXISTING (carried from iter-18, not iter-19): /app/backend/tests/test_phase9_1_updates_native.py is stale because VERSION.txt was bumped to 32.0.0-alpha.11 in iter-17 while the fixture still pins latest_version=31.1.0 -> check/download return 'no_update_available' and the phase 9.1 tests assert installed_version=='31.0.0'. Failures: TestCheck.test_check_with_fixture_returns_update_available, TestDownload.{success, sha256_mismatch, invalid_manifest_sha256_empty, invalid_manifest_sha256_short}, TestLauncherCheck (expects 'installed_ver = 31.0.0'), and TestApply.* (depend on a successful download). Fix options: (a) bump fixture manifest above current VERSION.txt, or (b) refactor phase 9.1 tests to read VERSION.txt and pin fixture to current+1.
PRE-EXISTING (carried from iter-18): TestRegressionSpotChecks expects /api/native/sync/status in {200,401,403} but live returns 404 — unrelated to updates/persistence.

ROLLOUT NOTE for main agent: alpha.11 customers will see the Setup Wizard ONE FINAL TIME after upgrading to alpha.12 because their prior system_config.json lived in _MEIxxxx and was wiped when alpha.11 closed. From alpha.12 onward, state will live in USER_DATA_DIR and survive reopens. Consider documenting this in release notes.
critical_code_review_comments: The new `if getattr(sys, 'frozen', False):` block in launcher._load_env() is correctly placed at the TOP of the function — BEFORE _bootstrap_env_from_template, _quarantine_dev_seed_if_present, and the dotenv load — so any subsequent `from native.config import config_manager` will resolve DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH against the pinned USER_DATA_DIR path.

USER_DATA_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) is called BEFORE os.environ.setdefault, eliminating the race where ConfigManager.save_config() (which calls self.config_path.parent.mkdir) might fire before the dir exists. Verified by TestUserDataDirCreated.

setdefault semantics are correct — explicit BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH or BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT in env (e.g. installer-managed deployments, MSI per-machine installs that pin to ProgramData) is preserved. Verified by TestFrozenRespectsOverride (3 cases including partial-override mixed).

DEV mode is fully untouched: when sys.frozen is absent OR explicitly False, _load_env() leaves BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH and BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT alone, so the sandbox keeps using /app/backend/native/system_config.json and existing dev tests don't regress. Verified by TestDevModeUnchanged (3 cases).

End-to-end persistence verified: after save_config() with setup_complete=True, an importlib.reload(native.config) (the closest in-process proxy for a process restart) reads back setup_complete=True with the same instance_id, master admin email, license key, and user list — proving the Setup Wizard would NOT re-run.

paths.{data_root,local_trivia,assets,generated} all derive from BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT via native.config._default_data_root(), so generated update bundles, trivia packs, and assets ALSO now persist under USER_DATA_DIR/app instead of _MEIxxxx — fix scope is broader than just system_config.json. Verified by TestEndToEndPersistence.test_paths_under_data_root_use_user_data_dir.

ENHANCEMENT (informational, not a blocker): _quarantine_dev_seed_if_present() reads BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH from env with a BACKEND_DIR fallback — in frozen mode this now correctly points at USER_DATA_DIR/system_config.json after the new block runs. The dev-seed instance_id 75d181a8-… cannot realistically appear in USER_DATA_DIR (only the alpha.6/.7 builds shipped it bundled), so this is a near no-op in practice. Consider a follow-up to also quarantine if cfg_path PARENT contains a stale system_config.dev-seed.json marker from a prior quarantine pass.

ENHANCEMENT (informational): native.config.DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH is a module-level constant evaluated at import time. If any future code path imports native.config BEFORE launcher._load_env() runs (e.g. a new sidecar entrypoint, or a test that imports server.py first), the config path will still resolve to BACKEND_DIR. Document this ordering invariant in launcher.py's module docstring AND add a defensive `import native.config; importlib.reload(native.config)` at the end of _load_env() to harden against future entrypoint rearrangements.

ENHANCEMENT (informational): TestBackendHealthy reads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with a /app/frontend/.env fallback — this matches the testing convention but could be hoisted into conftest.py as a shared `base_url` fixture used by all backend test files for consistency with iter-18's tests.

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15 iter-19 assertions). Phase 9.1 regression suite shows the same 7 pre-existing failures + 4 dependent errors carried over from iter-18 (VERSION.txt drift to 32.0.0-alpha.11), NOT caused by iter-19 changes.

test_credentials: N/A — iter-19 fix does not touch auth; uses tmp_path-scoped USER_DATA_DIR and never logs in.
seed_data_creation: None — all config files / users / license keys created during TestEndToEndPersistence are written under tmp_path and torn down automatically by pytest.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-19 persistence fix is fully validated by /app/backend/tests/test_launcher_persistence_iter19.py (15 PASS). Key invariant: launcher._load_env() MUST run BEFORE any `import native.config` (including via `from server import app`), otherwise DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH freezes at the wrong value. Tests use monkeypatch.setattr(sys, 'frozen', True, raising=False) + monkeypatch.setattr(launcher, 'USER_DATA_DIR', tmp_path/...) + importlib.reload(native.config) — same pattern is reusable for any future test that needs to flip the frozen flag. The pre-existing phase 9.1 stale-fixture issue (VERSION.txt=32.0.0-alpha.11 vs fixture 31.1.0) is STILL outstanding from iter-18 — main agent should either bump the fixture or refactor phase 9.1 tests; until then expect ~7 failed + 4 errored when re-running test_phase9_1_updates_native.py.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: In PyInstaller-frozen mode, sys._MEIPASS / BACKEND_DIR resolves to a per-launch _MEIxxxx temp directory that PyInstaller extracts on each launch and Windows wipes when the process exits. The launcher had already pinned DB (MONTYDB_DATA_DIR), JWT_SECRET, DEFAULT_HOST_PASSWORD, and ADMIN_MASTER_PASSCODE into USER_DATA_DIR (%LOCALAPPDATA%\BIGHat\data), but did NOT set BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH or BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT — so native.config.ConfigManager defaulted to BACKEND_DIR/native/system_config.json which is the _MEIxxxx temp dir. system_config.json holds setup_complete=True, the license key, master admin user records (with bcrypt hashes), and the paths block — losing it on every close meant ConfigManager() instantiated with setup_complete=False on every reopen, which forced the Setup Wizard to run from scratch. The customer (alpha.11 user) hit this on EVERY relaunch: re-enter license key, re-create master admin, re-pick venue/location — exactly the symptom reported. FIX VERIFIED in iter-19: launcher._load_env() now has a `if getattr(sys, 'frozen', False):` block at the very top that (a) creates USER_DATA_DIR with parents=True, exist_ok=True, then (b) os.environ.setdefault('BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH', str(USER_DATA_DIR / 'system_config.json')) and (c) os.environ.setdefault('BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT', str(USER_DATA_DIR / 'app')). Because native.config.DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH = Path(os.environ.get('BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH', …)) is evaluated at import time, and launcher.py inserts /app/backend at sys.path[0] BEFORE calling _load_env(), the env vars are set before the FIRST import of native.config (via _ensure_data_dirs() -> `from native.config import config_manager`). REPRODUCTION STEPS (frozen-mode simulation, hermetic): (1) monkeypatch.setattr(sys, 'frozen', True, raising=False); (2) monkeypatch.setattr(launcher, 'USER_DATA_DIR', tmp_path/'BIGHat'/'data'); (3) clear BIGHAT_CONFIG_PATH/BIGHAT_DATA_ROOT from env; (4) call launcher._load_env(); (5) importlib.reload(native.config); (6) cm1 = ConfigManager(); cm1.save_config({setup_complete:True, ...}); (7) del cm1; importlib.reload(native.config); (8) cm2 = ConfigManager(); assert cm2.config['setup_complete'] is True AND cm2.config['instance_id'] == cm1's instance_id. MITIGATION FOR EXISTING ALPHA.11 INSTALLS: their prior state was in _MEIxxxx and is unrecoverable — they will see the Setup Wizard exactly once after upgrading to alpha.12, then never again. Optional improvement: ship a one-time migration that scans for a system_config.json in the legacy alpha.11 install path and copies it to USER_DATA_DIR before _load_env() runs (but realistically none exists because _MEIxxxx is wiped, so this is moot).
